title: Print Scaling Is Where Resort Wear Manufacturing Gets Serious
excerpt: A camp shirt, wrap dress, boardshort, and UPF50+ rash guard cannot use the same print scale without adjustment.
author: Aloha and Co

sections.title: Not One Repeat for Every Garment
paragraphs: Resort wear prints need to be scaled by garment type. A large botanical repeat may look strong on a maxi dress but oversized on kids swimwear. A small print may work on a polo but feel flat on a camp shirt.

sections.title: Placement Matters
paragraphs: Plackets, collars, pockets, waistbands, sleeves, and side seams all affect how the artwork lands. Good production checks the print before cutting, not after the finished sample looks wrong.

sections.title: The Role of Sampling
paragraphs: Sampling is where artwork, fabric, and construction meet. A 10-15 day sample window gives room to verify color, repeat scale, drape, and finishing before bulk production begins.
